storm.summary_layperson: Radar is clear with no storms in the area tonight; the only active NWS notice is a minor coastal flood statement for the evening high tide.
storm.local_effect_blurb: Sound water at 73°F is 7°F cooler than shore air, producing a weak marine layer that could moderate storms on an active day — but there are none approaching tonight.
storm.detail_html:
No active storm threat for Riverside this evening. Radar shows zero convective cells in the approach sector and CAPE is 0 J/kg — the atmosphere has no instability to sustain thunderstorm development. The only NWS notice in effect is a minor Coastal Flood Statement (onset 9 PM, expires midnight) tied to the astronomical tide cycle, not any storm activity.

The OKX Area Forecast Discussion is entirely benign for today and tonight. The only convective mention is vague long-range language about "a few chances for showers or thunderstorms late week into the weekend" from passing shortwaves — nothing affecting the next 24 hours.

Coastal stabilization: Long Island Sound water is at 73°F against 81°F shore air, a 7°F differential that produces a weak marine stable layer. This layer offers moderate-likelihood protection against storms approaching from the SW–NW — but with no inbound cells, the mechanism isn't in play tonight. The 10°F delta required to flag the protective mechanism as active is not met.

Shear is 24 kt NNW, within the 20–35 kt gray zone between protection and punch-through. On a high-CAPE day that shear would warrant close watching. With CAPE at zero, no organized convection can develop, and risk is firmly low. The AFD's first potential convective chances appear late this week into the weekend via passing shortwaves.

wind.regime_label: Light southerly gradient, sea breeze not organized

hourly.shift_note: SSE, light and variable near the edges
veering to S-SSW, best pressure of the evening
steady S, clean up the cove axis

wind.tactics_blurb: Light SE-to-S gradient at 5–7 kt through the race window; pressure slightly favors the west side on the SSE shifts, evening out as direction matures toward true S by 18:00–19:00. Stay center with a slight west lean — oscillations of 10–20° expected, no clean persistent shift to commit to.
wind.tactics_detail_html:
Regime: B-light (gradient-only, light southerly). The sea-breeze two-step gate: deltaT = 7.2°F meets the ≥5°F thermal potential threshold, but the ensemble fails the second gate — coastal_pct = 0.38 (threshold 0.6) with no subregion above 0.5. Classifying as B-light: synoptic gradient, southerly, below 8 kt authoritative (ensemble mean 6.2 kt). RYC reads SE 130° at 4.9 kt (10-min avg 4.4 kt, gust 7.5 kt); the broader coastal authoritative stations run S–SSE at 6–8 kt; LI south shore stations show S at 5–11 kt. NWS forecast "SE 5 mph" is in good agreement.

HRRR/obs flag at 17:00 EDT: HRRR f02 shows an anomalous E 95° at 8.1 kt, inconsistent with current coastal obs (SSE 130–180°, ensemble median 170°). HRRR then swings to SSW 199° by 18:00, likely modeling a delayed sea-breeze front arrival. The obs-corrected SSE ~160° is used as the 17:00 baseline. The direction-correction block shows a 17° model/obs divergence (obs more veered) but the maturation correction was not triggered — coastal_pct is below the sea-breeze active gate. Direction agreement across 29 stations is only 0.55, reflecting a genuinely fragmented field: coastal stations are SSE/S while inland stations show residual NW/W flow from earlier in the day.

Cove geography: at SE 130° (current RYC), Greenwich Point (Tod's Point) partially shelters the east shore of the course — lighter and shiftier there, pressure on the west side. As the direction matures toward true S (180°) by 18:00–19:00, the flow lines up with the cove's N–S axis for the cleanest, steadiest racing of the evening. Favor the center-to-west portion of the course early; expect more uniform pressure by 19:00.

Shift character: B-light gradient with no frontal features (pressure steady at 1021 hPa, AFD clear for tonight). Expect mild oscillations of 10–20° around the mean SSE→S axis — not the clean tactical oscillations of a mature sea breeze, but the low-amplitude wandering of a light gradient day. No backdoor risk: July 12 is outside the Apr–mid-Jun seasonal window; KBOS/KPVD/KGON are all SSE/ESE, not NE.

At 5–7 kt all one-design classes are in the marginal-to-sweet-spot range. B-light gradient days don't collapse abruptly at sunset like a thermal sea breeze — the gradient softens 1–2 kt as the nocturnal boundary layer stabilizes. The 20:00 hour (near sunset 8:19 PM) is forecast at 5 kt; last race starts after 20:00 may tip into drifter territory (4 kt) by the finish.

data_caveats: WLIS buoy 44040 wind obs 75 min old, SST obs 102 min old — sea-breeze depth confidence reduced.
Station network direction agreement low (0.55) — wind field fragmented between coastal S/SSE and inland NW/W residuals; HRRR 17:00 direction discarded in favor of obs.
